editorial_thesis: Today’s strongest practical signal is not a new agent surface: it is the operating discipline around who can change production systems, how success is priced, and which measurements can be trusted.

topic_groups.title: Outcome Economics + Agent Governance
topic_groups.selection_rationale: Fresh primary guidance turns agent adoption from a model-selection discussion into an accepted-outcome, spend-control, and permission-design exercise; this lane was not covered in the recent delivery-telemetry dossier.

sources.title: A scorecard for the AI age
sources.why_selected: It defines useful intelligence per dollar around completed work, total cost, dependability, and scaling value.
sources.usefulness: Use it to replace token-price comparisons with a Buildn workflow scorecard.

sources.title: How to manage AI investments in the agentic era
sources.why_selected: It connects outcome ROI to access, tool, action, approval, and capacity controls.
sources.usefulness: Use it as a rollout checklist for scheduled and client-facing agents.

topic_groups.title: Design → Production Loop
topic_groups.selection_rationale: Design delivery has been lightly covered, and two fresh releases make reviewable, scoped changes possible from prototyping and design contexts without treating designers as unrestricted engineers.

sources.title: Workflow Lab: Deploying Designs Directly with Figma Make
sources.why_selected: It lays out a concrete branch-to-PR path for small accessibility and polish fixes, with engineer review retained.
sources.usefulness: Adapt the scope and approval gates for Buildn/client web delivery.

sources.title: ProtoPie 10.3.0
sources.why_selected: The release adds a Studio MCP Server, Figma-to-Studio paste, and local quick preview for interactive prototypes.
sources.usefulness: Try agent-assisted interaction prototyping before committing implementation time.

topic_groups.title: Measurement Integrity
topic_groups.selection_rationale: This is a deliberate coverage gap: fresh evidence challenges both coding benchmarks and agentic-commerce headlines, while GitHub’s GA quality product makes operational gates immediately actionable.

sources.title: GitHub Code Quality becomes generally available
sources.why_selected: GA adds organization targeting, dashboards, ruleset coverage enforcement, scoring, and APIs, alongside a clear cost change.
sources.usefulness: Use it to price and enforce quality gates across active client repositories.

sources.title: Separating Signal from Noise in Coding Evaluations
sources.why_selected: The audit estimates roughly 30% of SWE-Bench Pro tasks are broken and withdraws a prior recommendation.
sources.usefulness: Treat external benchmark rankings as a hypothesis; retain task-level acceptance tests.

sources.title: How Agentic Is Agentic Commerce?
sources.why_selected: Population-scale x402 analysis shows settlement count alone can be manufactured and cannot establish independent adoption.
sources.usefulness: Apply provenance and counter-metrics before using agent-economy figures in strategy or client claims.
